qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: JWT none-algorithm verification bypass accepts attacker-forged unsigned tokens
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding identifies the same vulnerable function and root cause as the advisory: `Sources/JSONWebSignature/JWS+Verify.swift` returns `true` for `SigningAlgorithm.none` in `JWS.verify(key:)`, and `JWT.verify(...)` reaches that helper.

selected_finding.title: JWT none-algorithm verification bypass accepts attacker-forged unsigned tokens

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The selected finding matches the advisory's exact vulnerable code path and the copied PoC demonstrated end-to-end exploit success: an unsigned `alg:none` JWT with attacker-chosen claims was accepted during verification even when a trusted verification key was supplied.
evidence: The advisory and finding both identify `Sources/JSONWebSignature/JWS+Verify.swift` as the root cause: `JWS.verify(key:)` immediately returns success when the protected header algorithm is `none`.

The finding's exploit path through `repo/Sources/JSONWebToken/JWT+Verification.swift:144` matches the advisory's affected high-level API `JWT.verify(jwtString:senderKey:)`, so the same vulnerable verification flow is implicated.

The judge-run PoC at `/Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-Run/advisory_f65df3d0/.judge/poc_runs/jwt_signature_verification_bypass_none_algorithm/run.log` produced `FORGED_ACCEPTED attacker true` and `SIGNED_TAMPER_REJECTED JWTError`, showing that a forged unsigned token was accepted while a tampered signed token was still rejected.

poc_executions.observed_effect: The copied Docker-based reproduction accepted an attacker-forged unsigned JWT and printed `FORGED_ACCEPTED attacker true`; a tampered ES256 token still failed verification and printed `SIGNED_TAMPER_REJECTED JWTError`.
